payload.intent: Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: The PRD's exact-gap description matches current source.
The persistence, unique-index, and migration plan is technically feasible with existing infrastructure.
Two promise-contract gaps (reserve-failure semantics, canonical-key field set) should be closed at PRD level before TDD.

criteria: Verified the cited submit function and job_id minting in source
Verified schema and absence of idempotency column
Verified a real forward-only migration framework exists
Verified named regression tests and Popen stub fixture exist
evidence: REVISE: scope, direction, and feasibility are sound — accept the PRD's intent but require two bounded additions to the promise contracts before advancing to TDD.

Add to P1/P3: define what a retry observes when the first attempt reserved an idempotency token but the worker failed to launch or never produced a result; a dead reservation must not permanently block re-launch.
Add to P2: explicitly enumerate which request fields feed the derived canonical hash (raw caller args vs config-resolved values) so config drift between retries does not silently spawn a duplicate.
Confirm at implementation that the unique index permits multiple NULL tokens (SQLite default) so migrating legacy rows with NULL tokens does not fail.
Verified exact gap, schema, migration framework, regression tests, and Popen stub all exist as the PRD assumes.
confidence.rationale: All structural PRD claims were verified directly against source (gap, schema, migration framework, regression tests, Popen stub), so feasibility is high-confidence. Confidence is held below 0.9 because the accept/revise judgment depends on whether the supervisor treats reserve-failure semantics and canonical-key definition as PRD-level (my position) or defers them to the next gate, and because no tests were run in this review gate.

payload.content: PRD correctly identifies the real gap (codex_supervisor_stdio.py:1715 mints a fresh workflow-uuid per call; Popen at :1770 launches unconditionally) and is feasible: dual_agent_workflow_jobs table exists, a forward-only migration registry supports adding an idempotency_token column + unique index, regression tests and a FakePopen stub already exist. REVISE: two promise-contract gaps must be pinned before TDD — (1) reserve-failure semantics for a token reserved before a worker that fails/never launches, which otherwise defeats retry-safety; (2) the exact field set feeding the derived canonical hash, since the payload mixes raw args with config-resolved values that can drift between retries.

assumptions_to_verify: S1 has actually landed (migration framework and durable tail cursor commit d989c09 suggest yes, but not explicitly asserted in the packet)
The derived canonical hash will be computed over caller-supplied logical args, not the config-resolved payload
The unique index will be a plain UNIQUE index permitting multiple NULLs so legacy-row migration does not fail
contradictions_checked: Claimed gap vs source: confirmed (codex_supervisor_stdio.py:1715/:1770)
Claimed migration capability vs source: confirmed (schema_migrations.py registry)
Claimed regression tests exist vs source: confirmed (test_dual_agent_workflow_driver.py:768/:817)
Claimed testable boundary with fake Popen vs source: confirmed (FakePopen :774)
missing_evidence: No definition in the PRD of reserve-then-fail recovery behavior
No enumeration of which request fields are included in the derived canonical hash
No confirmation that config-resolved payload fields are excluded from or normalized in the canonical key
critical_review.strongest_objection: The PRD frames itself as a durability/retry-safety slice yet its promise contracts (P1/P3) never define what a retry sees when the first attempt reserved an idempotency token but the worker failed or never launched; if a dead reservation permanently shadows the token, retries return a failed job and never relaunch, which defeats the entire stated goal.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If the supervisor's gate policy treats failure-path semantics and canonical-key field selection as out-of-scope for prd_review and properly owned by the TDD/implementation-plan gate, then the PRD is acceptable as-is and the decision shifts from REVISE to ACCEPT-with-notes.

assumptions_to_verify: Canonical hash excludes non-deterministic/volatile fields so retries of the same logical submit collide

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

Out-of-scope deferral of append idempotency is consistent with grill finding G4
S1 foundation (job table) is present so S2 can build on it

S1 table dual_agent_workflow_jobs exists without idempotency_token
Referenced test and doc artifacts exist
Five promise contracts carry boundary and allowed/forbidden outcomes

confidence.rationale: Gate premise and S1 foundation verified directly against source (table schema, submit function, Popen, referenced files all confirmed). PRD contracts are complete and well-formed. Residual uncertainty is the canonical-hash field-set, which downstream TDD/implementation gates must resolve but does not block PRD acceptance.

PRD's five promise contracts are well-formed with public boundary and allowed/forbidden outcomes; out-of-scope correctly defers append idempotency and S3a/S3b/S5. ACCEPT with three non-blocking refinements for the TDD gate.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Plan exercises the supervisor_tool_api public boundary for all four primary RED cases
Required fixtures (fake Popen, migration test file, named regression tests) exist in the tree
Derived-token independence is the only material missing coverage

criteria: Public-boundary RED for all primary promises: met
Required fixtures exist and are reusable: verified in source
Migration boundary correct for legacy DBs: verified
Full negative coverage for derived tokens: NOT met
evidence: accept: TDD plan may advance to implementation_plan/execution
required: add a test that two materially different legacy payloads without client_token produce distinct jobs (derived-token independence)
required: migration test must assert the unique idempotency index actually rejects a duplicate non-null token, not merely that the column/index were added
confidence.rationale: Gap and fixtures verified directly in source; all grill findings corroborated. Confidence capped below 0.9 because one substantive coverage gap (derived-token independence) remains and no tests have been executed yet (plan stage).

payload.content: TDD plan for idempotent detached submit reviewed against live source. Gap confirmed (workflow-uuid minted per call, Popen before row insert). Plan hits public tool boundary, asserts launch count via existing FakePopen, targets real forward-migration mechanism and existing regression tests. One real coverage gap: no derived-token independence (different legacy payload -> different job). Accept to advance with that test required at implementation.

assumptions_to_verify: Canonical request hash excludes non-semantic/volatile fields so retries dedupe
Unique index is partial (WHERE idempotency_token IS NOT NULL) so legacy NULL rows coexist
Reattach response surfaces current status, not just job_id
contradictions_checked: Claimed gap vs source: confirmed, submit mints uuid with no dedup
Claimed migration coverage vs reality: confirmed run_forward_migrations + test_schema_migrations.py exist
Claimed FakePopen harness vs reality: confirmed at lines 774-780
PRD 'reserve before Popen' vs current ordering: current code launches before insert, so requirement is real
missing_evidence: A planned test asserting derived tokens differ for different logical payloads
Explicit assertion that the unique index rejects a duplicate non-null token
Confirmation of which request fields participate in the canonical hash
critical_review.strongest_objection: The plan verifies different-explicit-tokens-stay-independent (P4) but never verifies the derived-token analog: two materially different legacy payloads without client_token must yield different jobs. Canonical-hash scope bugs (over-collapse or over-discriminate) would pass the current plan undetected.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If the team treats derived-token independence as fully out of scope (pure same-payload dedupe only) and the canonical hash is trivially the whole payload, the objection weakens toward low; conversely, evidence that callers vary volatile fields between retries would raise it to high and warrant REVISE.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Plan's described gap matches current source exactly
Plan file targets (state.py, schema_migrations.py, codex_supervisor_stdio.py, two test files) all exist and fit the described patterns
Atomic reserve is feasible with SQLite unique index + existing _write_lock
Plan does not specify rollback/relaunch on reserved-token launch failure

criteria: All plan claims verified against source: PASS
Migration approach fits existing pattern: PASS
Regression tests exist and are reusable for race/dedup: PASS
Failure-path semantics of atomic reserve fully specified: FAIL (must fix in execution)
Tests run green: N/A for plan gate
evidence: ACCEPT the implementation_plan with execution-time revision: plan is sound and source-accurate
Require implementer to define reserve-rollback or relaunch behavior when a reserved token's worker launch fails (OSError path at codex_supervisor_stdio.py:1777) to avoid permanently wedging a token
Atomicity via SQLite unique idempotency_token index plus existing _write_lock is sufficient for the in-process thread-pool race test in the TDD
Migration must follow existing version-based forward-only pattern in supervisor/schema_migrations.py using a PRAGMA table_info guard
confidence.rationale: Every factual claim in the plan was verified directly against source (file:line evidence) and holds; the plan is complete, traceable, and the atomicity approach is sound. Confidence held below 0.9 because the core atomic path has one unspecified failure-mode (launch-failure token wedge) and this is a plan review, not a tested execution, so no green test evidence exists yet.

payload.content: Implementation plan for idempotent detached submit verified against source: the gap (fresh workflow-<hex> id minted per call, DB insert after Popen, no client_token/dedup), file targets, migration pattern, and regression tests all match reality. Plan is complete and traceable (P1-P5). Accept with one required execution-time fix: specify reserve-rollback/relaunch semantics when a reserved token's launch fails, otherwise a launch failure can permanently wedge a token.

assumptions_to_verify: Concurrent race test is in-process (thread pool) so _write_lock + unique index suffices — TDD confirms thread pool
Returning a terminal/failed job under an existing token is acceptable per PRD P1 'current job status' rather than requiring relaunch
Canonical hash over the existing sort_keys payload is stable across retries (no hidden nonce) — confirmed payload has none

Plan claims migration needed because CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS won't alter old DBs; confirmed against schema_migrations.py pattern
missing_evidence: No statement in plan/PRD/grill findings on rollback or relaunch when a reserved token's subprocess launch fails
No specification of the exact fields hashed into the canonical request key
No live run of the planned tests (expected for a plan gate)

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: The described gap matches verified source
Planning artifacts are sound and require no revision
S2 implementation and tests have not been performed
Existing regression tests referenced by the plan exist in tests/test_dual_agent_workflow_driver.py

idempotency_token/client_token absent from all .py source and test files
cited source lines confirm the unchanged mint-per-call behavior and schema
evidence: uv run pytest tests/test_dual_agent_workflow_driver.py tests/test_schema_migrations.py -q (NOT RUN)
confidence.rationale: High confidence in the factual finding (implementation absent) verified via git status, multi-file grep, and direct reads of the two cited lines. Residual uncertainty is only about supervisor intent (implement vs. gate), which is an escalation not a fact gap.

Planning artifacts (PRD/TDD/plan) are accurate, coherent, and implementable. However NO implementation or tests have been written and zero files changed, so the execution gate cannot be accepted as complete. Recommend implementing per the plan and re-gating with diff + regression evidence.

payload.intent: Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
workflow_claim_verification_failed

claims: submit is idempotent for explicit client_token and for derived no-token retries
concurrent same-token submit launches exactly one worker and persists one row
old DBs migrate to gain idempotency_token column and unique index idempotently
existing callers and workflow-<hex> job_id format preserved

criteria: derived token deterministic (no nondeterministic payload fields)
atomic reserve prevents double-spawn (BEGIN IMMEDIATE + unique index)
migration applies to old and fresh DBs
confidence.rationale: Implementation matches PRD/TDD/impl-plan exactly; atomicity, determinism, compatibility, and migration verified by reading source and test assertions. Confidence is capped below 0.9 solely because the suite was not executed in-gate, and the prior round failed on unverifiable claims, so I refuse to assert passing without execution.

payload.content: S2 idempotent detached submit is implemented to scope: optional client_token, run_id+canonical-payload-derived token when absent, unique idempotency_token column+partial index, atomic reserve-before-spawn (BEGIN IMMEDIATE under write lock), reattach without second subprocess, workflow-<hex> job_id and existing callers preserved. Event-append idempotency correctly deferred. Static review found no defects; targeted pytest suite was NOT executed in-gate (no approval / non-interactive), so test_status is unknown rather than passed.

assumptions_to_verify: _write_lock is the same lock guarding upsert and reserve so reserve+launch is serialized
ON CONFLICT idempotency_token COALESCE in upsert never nulls an existing token
S1 (durable workflow job table + result-file poll) has actually landed as assumed
contradictions_checked: job_id ordering: payload is built before job_id so derived token excludes job_id (confirmed stdio.py:1745-1784)
payload excludes client_token and generated paths (confirmed)
success vs reattach return shapes both expose status/job_id consistent with test assertions
fresh-DB uniqueness: inline CREATE TABLE lacks the unique index, but migration v2 creates it at State construction (confirmed by test_state_constructor_adds_workflow_job_idempotency_to_existing_db)
missing_evidence: Executed pass/fail output of the two targeted test files
Full suite regression (uv run --extra dev pytest -q) to catch unrelated breakage from the new upsert column ordering
critical_review.strongest_objection: Accepting a gate whose central guarantee (idempotency under retry/concurrency) is asserted without an executed test run repeats the exact failure mode of the previous round (workflow_claim_verification_failed). Static reasoning about SQLite concurrency can miss runtime issues (e.g., lock contention, row_factory assumptions, async/thread interaction).
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Executed output showing any of the 7 listed tests failing, or the full dev suite regressing, would move the decision from accept to revise.

payload.content:
Independently review the outcome_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

Corrective context from the previous round:
workflow_claim_verification_failed

assumptions_to_verify: _write_lock is the same lock guarding upsert and reserve so reserve+launch is serialized
ON CONFLICT idempotency_token COALESCE in upsert never nulls an existing token
S1 (durable workflow job table + result-file poll) has actually landed as assumed
contradictions_checked: job_id ordering: payload is built before job_id so derived token excludes job_id (confirmed stdio.py:1745-1784)
payload excludes client_token and generated paths (confirmed)
success vs reattach return shapes both expose status/job_id consistent with test assertions
fresh-DB uniqueness: inline CREATE TABLE lacks the unique index, but migration v2 creates it at State construction (confirmed by test_state_constructor_adds_workflow_job_idempotency_to_existing_db)
missing_evidence: Executed pass/fail output of the two targeted test files
Full suite regression (uv run --extra dev pytest -q) to catch unrelated breakage from the new upsert column ordering
critical_review.strongest_objection: Accepting a gate whose central guarantee (idempotency under retry/concurrency) is asserted without an executed test run repeats the exact failure mode of the previous round (workflow_claim_verification_failed). Static reasoning about SQLite concurrency can miss runtime issues (e.g., lock contention, row_factory assumptions, async/thread interaction).
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Executed output showing any of the 7 listed tests failing, or the full dev suite regressing, would move the decision from accept to revise.

tests assert the claimed behaviors

Confidence is capped below 0.9 solely because the suite was not executed in-gate, and the prior round failed on unverifiable claims, so I refuse to assert passing without execution.

claims: submit is idempotent for explicit client_token and for derived no-token retries
concurrent same-token submit launches exactly one worker and persists one row
old DBs migrate to gain idempotency_token column and unique index idempotently
existing callers and workflow-<hex> job_id format preserved
confidence_criteria: diff matches implementation plan file-by-file
derived token deterministic (no nondeterministic payload fields)
atomic reserve prevents double-spawn (BEGIN IMMEDIATE + unique index)
backward-compatible job_id and caller signature
migration applies to old and fresh DBs
tests assert the claimed behaviors
outcome.confidence_rationale: Implementation matches PRD/TDD/impl-plan exactly; atomicity, determinism, compatibility, and migration verified by reading source and test assertions. Confidence is capped below 0.9 solely because the suite was not executed in-gate, and the prior round failed on unverifiable claims, so I refuse to assert passing without execution.

assumptions_to_verify: _write_lock is the same lock guarding upsert and reserve so reserve+launch is serialized
ON CONFLICT idempotency_token COALESCE in upsert never nulls an existing token
S1 (durable workflow job table + result-file poll) has actually landed as assumed
contradictions_checked: job_id ordering: payload is built before job_id so derived token excludes job_id (confirmed stdio.py:1745-1784)
payload excludes client_token and generated paths (confirmed)
success vs reattach return shapes both expose status/job_id consistent with test assertions
fresh-DB uniqueness: inline CREATE TABLE lacks the unique index, but migration v2 creates it at State construction (confirmed by test_state_constructor_adds_workflow_job_idempotency_to_existing_db)
missing_evidence: Executed pass/fail output of the two targeted test files
Full suite regression (uv run --extra dev pytest -q) to catch unrelated breakage from the new upsert column ordering
critical_review.strongest_objection: Accepting a gate whose central guarantee (idempotency under retry/concurrency) is asserted without an executed test run repeats the exact failure mode of the previous round (workflow_claim_verification_failed). Static reasoning about SQLite concurrency can miss runtime issues (e.g., lock contention, row_factory assumptions, async/thread interaction).
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Executed output showing any of the 7 listed tests failing, or the full dev suite regressing, would move the decision from accept to revise.

decisions: accept
objections: Targeted pytest suite (tests/test_dual_agent_workflow_driver.py, tests/test_schema_migrations.py) was not executed in this gate due to lack of command approval in a non-interactive environment; test_status=unknown. Supervisor must run it before merge to retire residual risk.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
workflow_claim_verification_failed

claims: Implementation satisfies every in-scope intent item by source inspection
Tests are real and target the dedup/atomicity/migration behavior
test pass/fail is unverified because the suite was not run

criteria: All intent scope items present in diff
Reserve is atomic and short-circuits before subprocess launch
confidence.rationale: Static evidence is strong and the diff fully matches scope and TDD plan, but per the corrective context I refuse to claim tests pass without execution; the only residual risks are runtime-only (sqlite cross-thread) and would be caught by actually running the suite.

payload.content: S2 idempotent submit is correct by source inspection: optional client_token, run_id+canonical-payload derived token (excludes transport fields), unique partial index via migration v2, atomic BEGIN IMMEDIATE reserve that short-circuits before mkdir/Popen, job_id format preserved, event-append idempotency deferred as allowed. Tests map 1:1 to the TDD plan and are genuine, but I could not execute them (bash approval denied 4x), so the pass claim is unverified.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
workflow_claim_verification_failed

claims: client_token plumbed through submit and mcp.tool wrapper
derived token = sha256(run_id+canonical payload); explicit token = client:sha256
unique partial index on idempotency_token WHERE NOT NULL
reserve is atomic and returns existing job without launching on duplicate
job_id format workflow-<uuid> and legacy callers preserved

criteria: Reserve is atomic and reattach skips Popen
confidence.rationale: Static review gives high confidence the diff satisfies the scoped intent and the tests are well-formed and aligned to the TDD plan, but no test execution evidence exists and the corrective context is specifically about unverified workflow claims, so confidence is capped below the accept threshold.

payload.content: S2 idempotent submit is statically correct against the scoped intent: optional client_token, run_id+canonical-payload derived token, unique partial index via migration v2, atomic reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job (BEGIN IMMEDIATE), early reattach before Popen, and preserved workflow-<uuid> job_id. Tests map 1:1 to the TDD plan. However the regression suite could not be executed (test command blocked by permission gate), so the prior corrective failure workflow_claim_verification_failed is not yet cleared.

Verified normal success return omits 'reattached' matching test assertions
Verified reserve uses unique index + BEGIN IMMEDIATE so concurrent same-token submits serialize
missing_evidence: pytest pass/fail output for the 7 new/changed tests
full-suite run to confirm no regression (uv run --extra dev pytest -q)

payload.intent: Make detached-job submit IDEMPOTENT so a retry after a transport drop re-attaches to the SAME run instead of spawning a duplicate. This is durable-substrate S2; it assumes S1 has landed. Exact gap: submit_dual_agent_workflow_job currently mints a new workflow-<uuid> job_id on every call with no idempotency key or dedup. Scope: accept optional client_token; derive stable key from run_id plus canonical request hash when absent; persist unique token on the job; if token exists return current job without launching a second subprocess; make reserve atomic; keep existing callers and job_id format working; defer event append idempotency as an open question if it would balloon the diff. Non-goals: S3a terminal outcome, S5, S3b, or gate semantic changes.

payload.content: S2 idempotent detached-job submit is implemented to scope: optional client_token, derived run_id+canonical-payload key, atomic reserve-before-launch, preserved workflow-<hex> job id format, additive v2 migration with partial-unique index, and TDD-aligned tests. assumptions_to_verify: The five new tests actually pass under uv
_write_lock guards all writers to the shared connection so BEGIN IMMEDIATE check-then-insert is race-free in-process
contradictions_checked: Reserve-before-launch: confirmed, no double-spawn path found
Derived token excludes transport fields: confirmed payload built before job_id/paths
Migration alters old DBs: confirmed ALTER + partial unique index, not relying on CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
Different payloads/tokens stay independent: confirmed namespacing and tests
missing_evidence: Actual pytest run output (pass/fail counts)
Full success-path response shape for the first/created submit
Behavior of poll tool when reattaching to a failed-launch job
critical_review.strongest_objection: On Popen failure the reserved row persists with its idempotency_token; for the derived-key path the same payload always yields the same token, so retries reattach to a failed job and never relaunch, permanently poisoning that key for legacy callers.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing regression run, or evidence that a concurrent/cross-process path can bypass the write lock and double-spawn, would move this from accept to revise/deny.
